A car travels in a straight line along a road. Its distance '$x$' from a stop sign is given as a function of '$t$' by the equation $x(t)=\alpha t+\beta t^3$, where $\alpha=2.0$ m/s, $\beta=0.01$ m/s$^3$. Calculate the average velocity of the car in the time interval $t=2.00$ sec to $4.00$ sec.

A car driver is trying to jump across a path as shown in figure by driving horizontally off a cliff 'X' at the speed 10 m/s. When he touches peak Z (ignore air resistance), what would be speed? (use g = 10 m/s$^2$). Cliff X is 100m high, peak Z is 60m high.
